pr.title: Attempt to resolve the mystery of HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty
pr.author: lukechampine

summary: PR #2577 renamed ftCo_800C6AFC at 0x800C6AFC to HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty, moved that declaration out of ft_0C31.h, and cleaned many TU-local fake HSD_JObjSet* helpers by replacing callsites with baselib/jobj.h helpers or new WithMtxDirty variants. The PR author described this as an investigation into why fake JObj helpers in several TUs called a fighter-range symbol, concluding ftCo_800C6AFC was actually HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty emitted into the first callsite TU. Review accepted the cleanup while noting the macro/parentheses situation was weird and that the macro was fake.

key_files.role: Central header expanded with HSD_JObjSet* and WithMtxDirty helper variants; metadata shows the largest change at 156 added lines.
evidence_refs: changed_files.jsonl: src/sysdolphin/baselib/jobj.h added=156 deleted=0 hunks=16
PR body: introduced WithMtxDirty variants for cases Codex could not unify

what_changed: Renamed the 0x800C6AFC function in symbols and scratch metadata from ftCo_800C6AFC to HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty.
Changed the function definition in src/melee/ft/ft_0C31.c to the parenthesized form void (HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty)(HSD_JObj* jobj).
Deleted multiple TU-local fake HSD_JObjSet* helpers and replaced callsites with central baselib/jobj.h helpers or WithMtxDirty variants.
Deleted src/melee/gm/gmregtyfall.static.h, which had hosted fake tyfall JObj setters and wrappers around ftCo_800C6AFC.
Added or relied on baselib/jobj.h as the central owner for the JObj helper names; changed_files metadata shows 156 added lines in that header.
The PR had a decomp-dev bot report showing matched code decreased by 1380 bytes, including a broken match in ftKb_PrSpecialAirN_Anim and an unmatched-item regression in itkyasarin.

smart_moves.text: Centralized duplicated fake JObj helpers into baselib/jobj.h instead of maintaining per-TU clones that manually expanded asserts and routed through a fighter-named symbol.
evidence_refs: PR body: several TUs had fake HSD_JObjSet* helpers
diff: deletion of fake helpers in ftKb_SpecialNNs.c, ft_0C31.c, gmregtyfall.static.h, grcastle.c, itbombhei.static.h

smart_moves.text: Preserved a separate WithMtxDirty naming family for cases that could not be unified with existing HSD_JObjSet* helpers.
evidence_refs: PR body: for the rest, it introduced WithMtxDirty variants
diff: HSD_JObjSetTranslateXWithMtxDirty, HSD_JObjSetRotationYWithMtxDirty, HSD_JObjSetTranslateWithMtxDirtyOutOfLine callsites

smart_moves.text: Updated symbol metadata alongside source usage so callsites no longer needed to import a fighter header for JObj dirty behavior.
evidence_refs: diff: symbols.txt rename at 0x800C6AFC
diff: gmregtyfall.c removed <melee/ft/ft_0C31.h>
diff: ft_0C31.h removed ftCo_800C6AFC prototype

decomp_lessons.lesson: When many TUs contain local fake copies of the same HSD_JObjSet* inline helper, check whether a shared header inline or helper variant can express the operation before preserving duplicated generated code.
evidence_refs: PR body: several TUs had fake HSD_JObjSet* helpers
diff: fake helpers removed from multiple TUs and replaced with HSD_JObjSet* or HSD_JObjSet*WithMtxDirty

decomp_lessons.lesson: A function emitted in an unexpected TU/range can still be a header-owned helper if linker order and the first non-inlined callsite explain the placement; verify names against helper behavior before keeping subsystem-prefixed address names.
evidence_refs: PR body: Codex concluded ftCo_800C6AFC was actually HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty, just placed in the ft range when included
review: ribbanya suggested linker order and first callsite in ft_0C31.c could cause the compiler to emit the function body there

decomp_lessons.lesson: Do not treat decompiler-expanded asserts and local fake wrappers as final style when existing jobj.h helpers carry the same file/line/assert behavior.
evidence_refs: diff: fake_HSD_JObjSetTranslate manually used __assert("jobj.h", 916, "jobj") and __assert("jobj.h", 917, "translate") before replacement
diff: ftKb fake helpers with manual assert strings removed in favor of HSD_JObjGetScale, HSD_JObjSetScale, HSD_JObjSetRotationY

decomp_lessons.lesson: Macro spelling can affect MWCC output: this PR specifically observed different behavior when calling a macro wrapped in parentheses versus unwrapped.
evidence_refs: lukechampine issue comment: wrapping a macro in parens when calling it results in different behavior vs calling it unwrapped
diff: parenthesized calls and definition form such as (HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty)(jobj)

naming_conventions.name: HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty
evidence_refs: diff: symbols.txt rename from ftCo_800C6AFC to HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty at .text:0x800C6AFC
diff: scratches.txt rename for OK:0x800C6AFC entries
naming_conventions.notes: PR evidence supports this rename in the merged PR, but the intake does not independently validate global symbol correctness beyond the PR slice.

naming_conventions.name: HSD_JObjSet*WithMtxDirty
evidence_refs: PR body: introduced WithMtxDirty variants
diff: HSD_JObjSetTranslateXWithMtxDirty, HSD_JObjSetRotationYWithMtxDirty, HSD_JObjSetScaleXWithMtxDirty, HSD_JObjSetTranslateWithMtxDirtyOutOfLine
naming_conventions.notes: Used for variants that explicitly dirty matrices and could not all be unified with the normal helper spelling in this PR.

naming_conventions.name: HSD_JObjSetTranslateWithMtxDirtyOutOfLine
evidence_refs: diff: fn_800C69F4 and fn_800C6F34 changed fake_HSD_JObjSetTranslate callsites to HSD_JObjSetTranslateWithMtxDirtyOutOfLine
naming_conventions.notes: Name indicates the helper is intended to force an out-of-line mtx-dirty call; exact implementation is in jobj.h but loaded diff excerpt is truncated.

naming_conventions.name: fake_HSD_JObjSet*, fake_tyfall_HSD_JObjSet*, ftKb_JObjSet*
evidence_refs: diff: deleted fake/local helper definitions across ftKb_SpecialNNs.c, ft_0C31.c, gmregtyfall.static.h, grcastle.c, itbombhei.static.h
ribbanya comment: the macro is for sure fake
naming_conventions.notes: PR cleaned these up rather than endorsing fake helper naming.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use parenthesized macro/function spelling such as (HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty)(jobj) when macro expansion vs function-call emission changes codegen.
evidence_refs: lukechampine issue comment about wrapping a macro in parens changing behavior
diff: void (HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty)(HSD_JObj* jobj)
diff: (HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty)(jobj) in it_2725.c
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: Reviewer called the macro fake and accepted the cleanup despite weird parentheses; do not generalize this as preferred style without narrow objdiff evidence.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: For callsites where normal HSD_JObjSet* helpers inline differently, use WithMtxDirty or WithMtxDirtyOutOfLine variants to preserve the matrix-dirty call shape while removing fake local clones.
evidence_refs: PR body: introduced WithMtxDirty variants for remaining cases
diff: HSD_JObjSetTranslateWithMtxDirtyOutOfLine in ft_0C31.c
diff: HSD_JObjSetTranslateX/Y/ZWithMtxDirty and HSD_JObjSetScaleX/Y/ZWithMtxDirty in gm files
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: The PR text says attempts to unify everything failed; loaded evidence does not include detailed objdiff proof for each helper variant.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Replace manually expanded jobj.h assert blocks with header helpers when file/line/condition behavior matches.
evidence_refs: diff: fake_HSD_JObjSetTranslate used __assert("jobj.h", 916/917) before replacement
diff: gmregtyfall.static.h fake_HSD_ASSERT line numbers 932/947/962/660/776/791/806 deleted
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: Supported by cleanup diff and existing decomp standards, but this intake did not inspect the full added jobj.h implementation.

review_feedback.author: decomp-dev[bot]
review_feedback.text: Reported matched code decreased by 70.08% (-0.04%, -1380 bytes), with one broken match: main/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftKb_SpecialNNs ftKb_PrSpecialAirN_Anim from 100.00% to 98.60%, and one unmatched-item regression: main/melee/it/items/itkyasarin it_802EDDC0 from 99.28% to 99.11%.
evidence_refs: text_corpus.jsonl decomp-dev[bot] comment at 2026-06-04T00:30:08Z

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.text: Asked why the called function was wrapped in parentheses, and suggested that if the function is in jobj.h and ft_0C31.c is the first callsite by linker order and not fit to inline, the compiler may emit the function body to that TU.
evidence_refs: text_corpus.jsonl review by ribbanya at 2026-06-04T01:49:41Z state=APPROVED

review_feedback.author: lukechampine
review_feedback.text: Observed that wrapping a macro in parentheses when calling it results in different behavior than calling it unwrapped.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text.md issuecomment-4618500699

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.text: Stated the macro is for sure fake, but was fine merging the cleaned up duplicates for now even if it introduces weird parentheses.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text.md issuecomment-4618590043

follow_up_queries: Search current baselib/jobj.h for HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty, WithMtxDirty, and WithMtxDirtyOutOfLine to document the final helper family.
Search for remaining fake_HSD_JObjSet or fake_tyfall_HSD_JObjSet helpers after PR #2577.
Search for parenthesized HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty calls to see where macro-call spelling is still required.
Check whether ftKb_PrSpecialAirN_Anim and it_802EDDC0 regressions from the bot report were later repaired.
Search PRs mentioning ftCo_800C6AFC, HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty, or first callsite linker order for follow-up rationale.

The bot report shows regressions, but the PR was merged and later comments accepted cleanup; intake does not claim all functions matched after merge.
Reviewer intent is limited to the quoted approval/comment text; no broader endorsement of parenthesized macro style is inferred.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Centralize duplicated fake JObj helpers into jobj.h helpers when evidence supports the header inline
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2577 removed many TU-local fake HSD_JObjSet* helpers and replaced their callsites with HSD_JObjSet* or HSD_JObjSet*WithMtxDirty helper variants. Use this as evidence that repeated fake jobj.h assert/setter clones should be checked against the real baselib/jobj.h helper family before being preserved.
evidence_refs: PR body: several TUs had fake HSD_JObjSet* helpers
diff: fake helpers removed from ftKb_SpecialNNs.c, ft_0C31.c, gmregtyfall.static.h, grcastle.c, itbombhei.static.h

accepted_candidate_records.title: Parenthesized macro/function call spelling can affect MWCC output
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2577 used parenthesized HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty spelling and the author noted that wrapping a macro in parentheses when calling it changed behavior. Treat this as a narrow matching tactic requiring local evidence, not as general style.
evidence_refs: lukechampine issue comment: macro wrapped in parens behaves differently
diff: void (HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty)(HSD_JObj* jobj)
diff: (HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty)(jobj)

accepted_candidate_records.title: Use WithMtxDirty suffix for JObj setter variants that explicitly force mtx-dirty behavior
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2577 introduced HSD_JObjSet*WithMtxDirty and HSD_JObjSetTranslateWithMtxDirtyOutOfLine callsites for cases not unified with normal HSD_JObjSet* helpers.
evidence_refs: PR body: introduced WithMtxDirty variants
diff: HSD_JObjSetTranslateXWithMtxDirty and HSD_JObjSetTranslateWithMtxDirtyOutOfLine callsites

source_update_candidates.title: HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty symbol at 0x800C6AFC was renamed from ftCo_800C6AFC in PR #2577
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2577 changed config/GALE01/symbols.txt and scratches.txt so the function at .text/OK:0x800C6AFC is named HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty rather than ftCo_800C6AFC, and removed the ftCo_800C6AFC prototype from src/melee/ft/ft_0C31.h.
evidence_refs: diff: symbols.txt rename at .text:0x800C6AFC
diff: scratches.txt OK:0x800C6AFC entries renamed
diff: ft_0C31.h removed ftCo_800C6AFC prototype
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential path/symbol fact for curator review; intake does not promote it directly.

source_update_candidates.title: Document narrow use of parenthesized macro calls as matching tactics
source_update_candidates.text: Consider adding a note that parenthesized macro/function-like helper spelling may be used only with explicit local codegen evidence. PR #2577 observed that wrapping HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty in parentheses changed behavior, but review also called the macro fake and accepted it only as part of cleanup.
evidence_refs: lukechampine issue comment about wrapping a macro in parens
ribbanya comment: macro is for sure fake; okay merging cleaned up duplicates despite weird parentheses
diff: parenthesized HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty forms
source_update_candidates.reason: Could refine existing matching-tactics standards with a concrete cautionary example.

source_update_candidates.title: Prefer real header helpers over duplicated fake JObj helper clones
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2577 is evidence for extending header-inline guidance to repeated fake HSD_JObjSet* clones: when local fake helpers manually duplicate jobj.h assert lines and setter behavior, search for or add the appropriate baselib/jobj.h helper variant instead of keeping per-TU clones.
evidence_refs: PR body: several TUs had fake HSD_JObjSet* helpers
diff: multiple fake helpers removed and callsites replaced with HSD_JObjSet* helpers
source_update_candidates.reason: This is closely aligned with the loaded header-inlines standard but may be worth adding as a JObj-specific example.

rejection_notes: Do not promote a broad rule that all parenthesized macro calls are acceptable style; PR evidence frames this as weird and tied to a fake macro/codegen issue.
Do not claim the bot-reported regressions were resolved in this PR; loaded evidence only shows the report plus merge/approval.
Do not infer the full implementation details of the added jobj.h helpers because the loaded diff excerpt is truncated.
Do not claim Codex's conclusion independently proves historical compiler intent; it is PR-body evidence and was partially supported by reviewer speculation about linker order.
